7641.one_line_summary: ERC-20 extension with integrated revenue-sharing and burning mechanism for token holders.
7641.notes: Combines ERC-20 with snapshot-based revenue distribution and optional ERC-20 alt token support; burning mechanism provides intrinsic floor value.

7644.one_line_summary: Extends ERC-721 with time-limited unique name registry mechanism per token.
7644.notes: Adds naming system to NFTs with expiry dates ensuring uniqueness and preventing monopolization; compatible with ERC-721.

7649.one_line_summary: Embeds bonding curve-based liquidity into NFTs via tradable shares without modifying ERC-721.
7649.notes: Introduces TradeableShares tied to NFTs with customizable bonding curves and guaranteed creator fees; separate from NFT contract.

7651.one_line_summary: Standard for fractionally represented NFTs coexisting with fungible shares in unified contract.
7651.notes: Hybrid standard combining fractional and whole token semantics; distinct events to avoid ERC-20/ERC-721 conflicts; includes optional metadata and banking interfaces.

7652.one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension enabling guarantor role to guarantee NFT buyback at set prices.
7652.notes: Introduces guarantee mechanism where DAOs/guarantors offer price guarantees for NFT liquidity; supports multi-guarantor scenarios and sequence establishment.

7654.one_line_summary: Standardizes request-response communication (GET, POST, PUT, OPTIONS) for contracts.
7654.notes: Defines contract interaction protocol with self-describing parameter types; enables consistent client-contract communication across different implementations.

7656.one_line_summary: Factory for deploying generic services linked to contracts (accounts, NFTs) with deterministic addressing.
7656.notes: Extends token-bound accounts concept to any contract type; supports multiple linking modes via bytes12 parameter; uses ERC-1167 minimal proxy pattern.

7662.one_line_summary: Standard for AI Agent NFTs with encrypted prompts accessible only to token holder.
7662.notes: Extends ERC-721 with custom struct for AI agent metadata; recommends encrypted prompt storage with holder-specific decryption; supports dynamic variable injection in prompts.

7673.one_line_summary: Base256emoji encoding for addresses to improve human-readability and reduce spoofing attacks.
7673.notes: Replaces hexadecimal with 256-emoji mapping for addresses; squaring difficulty of spoofing addresses; addresses encoded as 20 emoji instead of 40 hex chars.

7674.one_line_summary: ERC-20 extension enabling single-transaction temporary approvals via transient storage.
7674.notes: Uses ERC-1153 TSTORE for ephemeral allowances; temporary approval consumed before persistent allowance; enables one-time approvals without leaving standing permissions.

7677.one_line_summary: Capability for ERC-4337 wallets to communicate with paymaster web services.
7677.notes: Defines JSON-RPC paymaster API (pm_getPaymasterStubData, pm_getPaymasterData) and wallet capability; enables app-specified paymaster via wallet_sendCalls.

7679.one_line_summary: Standard interface for smart accounts to expose nonce, calldata, signature encoding logic.
7679.notes: Allows vendor-agnostic UserOp construction without account-specific SDKs; IUserOperationBuilder provides getNonce, getCallData, formatSignature functions.

7681.one_line_summary: Dual-nature token combining ERC-20 fungible and ERC-1155 semi-fungible in single contract.
7681.notes: Unified token sync between standards; optional skip mechanism for smart contracts to avoid ERC-1155 minting overhead; implements both interfaces with distinct events.

7682.one_line_summary: EIP-5792 capability indicating wallet access to auxiliary funds beyond on-chain balance.
7682.notes: Enables advanced wallets to signal access to off-chain funds or multi-account assets; supports optional requiredAssets metadata for cross-chain/bridge flows.

7683.one_line_summary: Solver-facing interface for programmable intent protocols with resolver-based order decoding.
7683.notes: Standardizes how protocols describe orders to solvers via IResolver; defines steps, variables, payments, assumptions; supports cross-chain via ERC-7930 interoperable addresses.
